description: Innkeeper moves to cellar (clean — has cellar_access), then a long distraction-narrative turn buries a cellar→entrance jump — B1 only, no A1 (innkeeper is exempt from A1).
adversarial_note: Two adversarial pressures combined: (1) the innkeeper's cellar access means A1 cannot fire — if any violation appears it must be B1, confirming the constraint is path-based not access-based; (2) the distraction narrative buries the spatial transition inside descriptive prose where movement is incidental. A CVR=0 result may reflect elicitation failure if the LLM does not produce a location delta from the narrative — check elicitation_rate. The non-adjacent pair is cellar→entrance (cellar is adjacent only to main_hall).

condition_notes.A: ABox never updates. Oracle sees innkeeper at main_hall (baseline). main_hall→entrance is adjacent — B1 does not fire. Zero oracle violations expected under Condition A.
condition_notes.B: Turn 1 commits innkeeper→cellar. Turn 2: cellar→entrance fires B1 (cellar is not adjacent to entrance).
condition_notes.C: Same as B. Per-turn injection reflects innkeeper at cellar.

turns.prompt: The innkeeper heads down to the cellar to count the remaining stock.

The innkeeper counts bottles for a while, humming to himself, running a finger along the dusty shelves. A bell rings somewhere — customers at the front. He sets down his lantern, tucks the ledger under his arm, and steps out through the cellar door, crossing straight to the entrance to greet them.
